zoom_to_bounds.name: zoom_to_bounds
zoom_to_bounds.description: Zoom the map to a specific bounding box in WGS84 coordinates. This will save the user's current zoom location to history and navigate to the new bounds.

bounds.description: Bounding box in WGS84 format [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax]
bounds.title: Bounds

zoom_description.description: Complete message to display to the user while zooming, e.g. "Zooming to 39 selected parcels near Ohio"
zoom_description.title: Zoom Description

create_point_layer.name: create_point_layer
create_point_layer.description: Create a new point layer from coordinates. Use this when the user provides lat/lon coordinates and you need a layer to mark that location, buffer into a circle, or use as input for geoprocessing tools.

label.description: Human-readable label for the point, e.g. 'Rusumo Falls'
label.title: Label

search_location.name: search_location
search_location.description: Search for a location by name and return its bounding box and coordinates. Use this when the user mentions a place name and you need geographic coordinates for satellite imagery tools.

query.description: Place name to search for, e.g. 'Musanze district, Rwanda' or 'Kigali'
query.title: Query

display_satellite_layer.name: display_satellite_layer
display_satellite_layer.description: Display satellite imagery (true color) on the map for a specific area and date range. Searches Earth Search for the best Sentinel-2 scene and adds it as a visible tile layer. Use this when the user wants to see satellite imagery on the map.

bbox.description: Bounding box as 'west,south,east,north' in WGS84 coordinates, e.g. '29.44,-1.72,29.68,-1.50'
bbox.title: Bbox

date_from.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format, e.g. '2025-01-01'
date_from.title: Date From

date_to.description: End date in ISO 8601 format, e.g. '2025-01-31'
date_to.title: Date To

layer_name.description: Display name for the map layer, e.g. 'Musanze TCI Jan 2025'
layer_name.title: Layer Name

display_layer.name: display_layer
display_layer.description:
Display any public Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF on the map with a styled colormap.

    This is the GENERIC display tool. Use it after computing or identifying a spatial
    raster you want the user to SEE. Pair it with analytical tools that return a URL:
    1. Call the analytical tool (e.g. get_soil_properties returns iSDAsoil COG URL).
    2. Call display_layer with that URL + a style_hint that names the domain.

    The frontend renders the layer immediately and adds it to the user's layer panel
    so they can toggle it on/off.

    Style hints map to colormaps and value ranges defined in STYLE_PRESETS. Pick the
    one that matches the data's domain — soil_nitrogen for N maps, ndvi for NDVI,
    drought_severity for VCI, etc.

asset_url.description: Public HTTPS URL of a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF (COG). Examples: 'https://isdasoil.s3.amazonaws.com/soil_data/nitrogen_total/nitrogen_total.tif', 'https://earth-search.aws.element84.com/...../B04.tif'. GeoJSON URLs and S3 protocol URLs are not yet supported in this version.
asset_url.title: Asset Url

layer_name.description: Human-readable layer name shown in the layers panel, e.g. 'Soil Nitrogen — Cyampirita'
layer_name.title: Layer Name

style_hint.description: Style preset that picks the colormap and value range. One of: ndvi, ndwi, nbr, visual, ndvi_band, ndre_band, soil_nitrogen, soil_phosphorus, soil_potassium, soil_ph, soil_organic_carbon, soil_clay, soil_sand, anomaly_zscore, drought_severity, soil_moisture, evapotranspiration, temperature, rainfall, sar_backscatter_db.
style_hint.title: Style Hint

bbox.description: Bounding box of the area to display, as 'west,south,east,north' in WGS84. Used for auto-zoom.
bbox.title: Bbox

band_index.description: 1-based band index for single-band rasters (use 1 for most soil COGs and z-score rasters).
band_index.title: Band Index

display_geojson_layer.name: display_geojson_layer
display_geojson_layer.description:
Display inline GeoJSON polygons on the map with categorical fill colors.

    Use this when an analytical tool returns vector polygons (e.g. an insured
    parcel boundary with a composite_score, stress-zone clusters with severity,
    or AOI outlines). Pair with evaluate_insurance_trigger, find_stress_zones,
    or any tool that produces polygon evidence the user should SEE.

    The style_hint picks a categorical color ramp keyed off a property on each
    feature (composite_score, ndvi_mean, severity). The frontend renders the
    layer immediately and adds it to the user's layer panel.

    Pattern: compute (returns polygons + scored properties) → display_geojson_layer
    (paints them).

geojson.description: Inline GeoJSON FeatureCollection or Feature as a JSON string. Each feature should carry the property used by the style_hint's color_property (e.g. 'composite_score' for insurance_composite_score, 'ndvi_mean' for field_health, 'severity' for stress_zones).
geojson.title: Geojson

layer_name.description: Human-readable name for this vector layer in the layers panel.
layer_name.title: Layer Name

style_hint.description: Vector style preset. One of: insurance_composite_score, field_health, rgb_field_health, stress_zones, outline, water, flood_extent, similarity_score, food_security_ipc.
style_hint.title: Style Hint

bbox.description: Bounding box of the GeoJSON, as 'west,south,east,north' WGS84. Used for auto-zoom.
bbox.title: Bbox

compute_spectral_index.name: compute_spectral_index
compute_spectral_index.description: Compute a spectral index (NDVI, NDWI, or NBR) over an area and display it on the map with a colormap. Returns area statistics (mean, std, min, max). Use this for vegetation health analysis, water/flood detection, or burn severity mapping.

bbox.description: Bounding box as 'west,south,east,north' in WGS84 coordinates
bbox.title: Bbox

index.description: Spectral index to compute: 'ndvi' (vegetation health), 'ndwi' (water/flooding), or 'nbr' (burn severity)
index.title: Index

date_from.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format, e.g. '2025-01-01'
date_from.title: Date From

date_to.description: End date in ISO 8601 format, e.g. '2025-01-31'
date_to.title: Date To

layer_name.description: Display name for the map layer, e.g. 'NDVI Musanze Jan 2025'
layer_name.title: Layer Name

get_soil_moisture.name: get_soil_moisture
get_soil_moisture.description: Get soil moisture data from WaPOR for a specific location.

date_from.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Pass empty string '' for no lower bound.
date_from.title: Date From

date_to.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Pass empty string '' for no upper bound.
date_to.title: Date To

get_evapotranspiration.name: get_evapotranspiration
get_evapotranspiration.description: Get evapotranspiration data from WaPOR for a specific location.

date_from.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Pass empty string '' for no lower bound.
date_from.title: Date From

date_to.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Pass empty string '' for no upper bound.
date_to.title: Date To

include_components.description: If true, return ET broken down into transpiration + interception + evaporation. If false, return total ET only.
include_components.title: Include Components

get_food_security_alerts.name: get_food_security_alerts
get_food_security_alerts.description: Get FEWS NET food security alerts for Rwanda. Returns IPC phase classifications by district. When district-level data is present, the response includes a 'displayable_geojson' payload — pass it to display_geojson_layer with style_hint='food_security_ipc' to paint the districts with the standard IPC color scale (green=Minimal → dark red=Famine).

district.description: Rwanda district name to filter by. Pass empty string '' for all districts.
district.title: District

period.description: Reporting period: 'current' for the present situation, 'projected' for forecast. Empty string '' defaults to 'current'.
period.title: Period

predict_ndvi_from_sar.name: predict_ndvi_from_sar
predict_ndvi_from_sar.description: Predict NDVI from Sentinel-1 SAR backscatter using ML model.

target_date.description: Target date YYYY-MM-DD, OR empty string '' to use the most recent SAR scene.
target_date.title: Target Date

detect_water_bodies.name: detect_water_bodies
detect_water_bodies.description: Detect water bodies from Sentinel-1 SAR imagery. Returns water area, fraction, and polygon evidence in 'displayable_geojson' — call display_geojson_layer with style_hint='water' to paint the detected water on the map.

date.description: Date YYYY-MM-DD, OR empty string '' to use the most recent scene.
date.title: Date

detect_flood_extent.name: detect_flood_extent
detect_flood_extent.description: Detect flood extent by comparing two SAR images (before/after). Returns flood area, fraction, and polygon evidence in 'displayable_geojson' — call display_geojson_layer with style_hint='flood_extent' to paint the new flooded area on the map.

get_alos_l_band_stats.name: get_alos_l_band_stats
get_alos_l_band_stats.description: Get ALOS PALSAR L-band backscatter statistics for an area. When at least one year has tiles, the response includes 'displayable_layers' with the most-recent year's HH backscatter COG URL — pass it to display_layer with style_hint='sar_backscatter_db' to paint the L-band map (forest = bright, water = dark).

years.description: Comma-separated year list (e.g. '2020,2021,2022') OR empty string '' for all available years.
years.title: Years

get_alos_temporal_variation.name: get_alos_temporal_variation
get_alos_temporal_variation.description: Get ALOS PALSAR temporal variation analysis for an area.

years.description: Comma-separated year list (e.g. '2020,2021,2022') OR empty string '' for all available years.
years.title: Years

check_cygnss_availability.name: check_cygnss_availability
check_cygnss_availability.description: Check CYGNSS data availability for a region.

bbox.description: Bounding box as 'minLon,minLat,maxLon,maxLat', OR empty string '' to default to all of Rwanda.
bbox.title: Bbox

get_cygnss_soil_moisture.name: get_cygnss_soil_moisture
get_cygnss_soil_moisture.description: Get CYGNSS-derived soil moisture for a location.

days_back.description: Lookback window in days (typical: 90). Pass 0 to use the default of 90.
days_back.title: Days Back

resolution_km.description: Spatial resolution in km (9 or 36). Pass 0 to use the default of 9.
resolution_km.title: Resolution Km

get_cygnss_watermask.name: get_cygnss_watermask
get_cygnss_watermask.description: Get CYGNSS L-band water body detection mask for an area. Detects water UNDER vegetation canopy where C-band SAR fails. Returns water polygons in 'displayable_geojson' — call display_geojson_layer with style_hint='water' to paint the detected water on the map.

date.description: Date YYYY-MM-DD, OR empty string '' to use the most recent product.
date.title: Date

product.description: CYGNSS product name, OR empty string '' to use the default 'watermask_daily'.
product.title: Product

describe_user_raster.name: describe_user_raster
describe_user_raster.description: Describe a user-uploaded raster layer in plain language: bounds, area in hectares, band count, dimensions, file size, COG status, capture date if known. Performs a sanity check that this layer's geographic location is reasonable relative to other rasters in the same project (catches CRS errors where a Rwanda field gets georeferenced to Florida by a misconfigured drone export). Use this whenever the user asks ABOUT one of their uploaded rasters — drone orthophotos, NDVI rasters, multispectral tiffs. Do NOT use for satellite-data questions (use get_ndvi_stats or get_field_health for those).

layer_id.description: The layer_id of the user-uploaded raster (starts with 'L', visible in the project's layer panel). Example: 'LwQ6VWK64bvL'.
layer_id.title: Layer Id

compute_zonal_stats.name: compute_zonal_stats
compute_zonal_stats.description: Compute pixel statistics (mean, min, max, std, p10, p50, p90) over a polygon area within a user-uploaded raster, on a specified band. NoData pixels are excluded from the calculation and the percentage of valid pixels is reported. If polygon_geojson is empty, computes stats over the whole raster. Use this whenever you need numerical stats from a user's drone or NDVI raster — for example, "what's the average NDVI in this field?". Do NOT use for satellite stats (those have their own tools).

layer_id.description: The layer_id of the user-uploaded raster.
layer_id.title: Layer Id

polygon_geojson.description: A GeoJSON Polygon string defining the area to analyze (use this if the user drew a polygon or wants stats over a specific field). Pass empty string '' to analyze the whole raster.
polygon_geojson.title: Polygon Geojson

band.description: Which raster band to analyze (1-indexed). For typical 4-band drone NDVI exports, NDVI is in band 2. For dedicated single-band NDVI rasters, use band 1. Default to 1 if uncertain.
band.title: Band

interpret_raster_health.name: interpret_raster_health
interpret_raster_health.description: Interpret pixel data from a user-uploaded NDVI raster as a farmer-language verdict on field health, given a crop type, growth stage, and field polygon. Composes describe_user_raster (for metadata + CRS sanity) plus compute_zonal_stats (for pixel statistics) plus the agricultural crop calendar (to map capture date to growth stage) plus a curated NDVI threshold table (to convert numbers to verdicts). Returns a verdict (exceptional / healthy / moderate_stress / severe_stress) plus evidence and recommended action — NOT raw band statistics. ALWAYS use this when the user asks about the health of a field they have a drone NDVI or NDVI raster for. Do NOT use for satellite-only questions (use get_field_health for those).

layer_id.description: The layer_id of the user-uploaded NDVI raster (drone export, computed NDVI from satellite, etc.).
layer_id.title: Layer Id

crop.description: The crop in the field. Supported: 'maize', 'beans', 'rice', 'sorghum', 'wheat'. Pass 'unknown' if the user hasn't specified — defaults will be used.
crop.title: Crop

band.description: Which band of the raster contains NDVI (1-indexed). For typical drone exports with 4 bands [R, NDVI, NDRE, alpha], use band=2. For dedicated single-band NDVI rasters, use band=1. Default to 1 if uncertain.
band.title: Band

polygon_geojson.description: GeoJSON Polygon string defining the field boundary, OR empty string '' to evaluate the whole raster.
polygon_geojson.title: Polygon Geojson

captured_date.description: Date the imagery was captured, YYYY-MM-DD format. Pass empty string '' if unknown — today's date will be used as an approximation (less accurate for growth-stage inference).
captured_date.title: Captured Date

analyze_rgb_field.name: analyze_rgb_field
analyze_rgb_field.description: Analyze a user-uploaded RGB visual orthophoto for greenness and field coverage. Computes GRVI (Green-Red Vegetation Index) which is the only vegetation index derivable from RGB without a near-infrared band — about 70% as informative as true NDVI for distinguishing healthy from stressed canopy. Reports valid coverage in hectares (alpha-mask aware), GRVI mean / std / percentiles, and a coarse verdict (lush/healthy/moderate/sparse/bare). Use this for RGB-only drone orthos when the user asks about field coverage or visible greenness. ALWAYS prefer interpret_raster_health when the layer has a true NDVI band — true NDVI is more accurate. This tool is the honest fallback for RGB-only data.

layer_id.description: The layer_id of the user-uploaded RGB visual orthophoto (3-band ortho, no NIR).
layer_id.title: Layer Id

polygon_geojson.description: GeoJSON Polygon string defining the field area, OR empty string '' to analyze the whole raster.
polygon_geojson.title: Polygon Geojson

red_band.description: Which band contains the Red channel (1-indexed). For typical RGB drone orthos, Red is band 1. For BGR-ordered exports it may be band 3.
red_band.title: Red Band

green_band.description: Which band contains the Green channel (1-indexed). For typical RGB drone orthos, Green is band 2.
green_band.title: Green Band

read_pixel_at.name: read_pixel_at
read_pixel_at.description: Read the per-band raw pixel value at a specific lon/lat location in a user-uploaded raster. Validates the point falls inside the layer's bounds first — if the user clicks at Rwanda coordinates against a Florida-georeferenced raster, returns a clear "outside bounds" error instead of silently returning NoData. Use this when the user clicks a specific spot on the map and asks "what's the value here?". For statistics over an area, use compute_zonal_stats. For a verdict on the whole field, use interpret_raster_health or analyze_rgb_field.

layer_id.description: The layer_id of the user-uploaded raster.
layer_id.title: Layer Id

longitude.description: Longitude in WGS84 decimal degrees (e.g. 30.4245 for Rwanda).
longitude.title: Longitude

latitude.description: Latitude in WGS84 decimal degrees (e.g. -1.6970 for Rwanda).
latitude.title: Latitude

get_value_distribution.name: get_value_distribution
get_value_distribution.description: Compute the value distribution (histogram + percentiles) of a raster band over a polygon (or whole raster). Returns: bin edges, bin counts, percentiles (5/10/25/50/75/90/95), mean, std, min, max, mode-bin. Skips NoData. Use this when the user asks about the spread, distribution, or variability of pixel values — for example, "what's the NDVI distribution across this field?", "are values bimodal?", "what's the worst 10% of pixels?". For a single number, use compute_zonal_stats. For a verdict on the field, use interpret_raster_health.

layer_id.description: The layer_id of the user-uploaded raster.
layer_id.title: Layer Id

band.description: Which band to sample (1-indexed). For NDVI in 4-band drone exports use 2; for single-band NDVI use 1.
band.title: Band

polygon_geojson.description: GeoJSON Polygon string to constrain the histogram, OR empty string '' to sample the whole raster.
polygon_geojson.title: Polygon Geojson

bins.description: Number of histogram bins. Use 20 as a default for human-readable display; up to 100 for fine analysis.
bins.title: Bins

find_stress_zones.name: find_stress_zones
find_stress_zones.description: Find connected clusters of low-NDVI pixels in a user-uploaded NDVI raster — the spatial answer to "where is the stress in my field?". Returns a list of stress zones with each zone's center coordinate, area in hectares, mean NDVI inside the cluster, and severity label (severe/moderate/mild). Refuses non-NDVI rasters with a pointer to the right tool. Useful for: locating drought patches, finding diseased zones, prioritizing field walks. For a single overall verdict use interpret_raster_health; for a histogram use get_value_distribution.

layer_id.description: The layer_id of a user-uploaded NDVI raster (single-band float, or band 2 of a 4-band drone NDVI export).
layer_id.title: Layer Id

band.description: Which band contains NDVI (1-indexed). For single-band NDVI use 1; for typical 4-band drone NDVI export use 2.
band.title: Band

ndvi_threshold.description: Pixels with NDVI BELOW this threshold are considered stressed. Reasonable defaults: 0.30 for severe stress, 0.40 for moderate. For a vegetative-stage maize field expecting NDVI ~0.55-0.70, use 0.40 to flag clear stress patches.
ndvi_threshold.title: Ndvi Threshold

min_area_ha.description: Skip stress clusters smaller than this many hectares. Use 0.5 to filter out noise patches; use 0.1 for fine-grained analysis. Connected stress patches below this size are not reported.
min_area_ha.title: Min Area Ha

compare_rasters.name: compare_rasters
compare_rasters.description: Compare two user-uploaded NDVI rasters of the same field at different times to detect change. Method 3: per-pixel NDVI diff at downsampled resolution + crop-stage-aware expected delta + CHIRPS rainfall context. Returns: time interval in days, observed mean NDVI delta, expected delta given crop stage transitions, anomaly (observed - expected), rainfall between captures, area declining significantly, and a verdict (no_significant_change / expected_growth / mild_decline / drought_signature / harvest_pattern / suspicious_pattern). Refuses if bounds don't overlap or either layer is RGB-only. The keystone tool for insurance change detection.

layer_id_a.description: The first user-uploaded NDVI raster (typically the EARLIER flight or 'before' timepoint).
layer_id_a.title: Layer Id A

layer_id_b.description: The second user-uploaded NDVI raster (typically the LATER flight or 'after' timepoint). Must overlap with layer_id_a.
layer_id_b.title: Layer Id B

band.description: Which band contains NDVI (1-indexed). For single-band NDVI use 1; for typical 4-band drone NDVI export use 2.
band.title: Band

crop.description: The crop in the field — used to look up the expected NDVI delta between the two captures' growth stages. Supported: 'maize', 'beans', 'rice', 'sorghum', 'wheat'. Pass 'unknown' if no crop information.
crop.title: Crop

evaluate_insurance_trigger.name: evaluate_insurance_trigger
evaluate_insurance_trigger.description: Evaluate parametric insurance trigger conditions on a user's drone NDVI flights. Composes compare_rasters (change detection) + zonal stats on the 'after' raster (current absolute health) + a per-crop-stage threshold table. Computes a 0-100 composite_score across 4 weighted signals (absolute health, NDVI decline vs expected, area declining significantly, drought context from rainfall) and returns triggered=True if score >= 60. Returns triggered (bool), composite_score (0-100), per-signal status with thresholds, payout_recommendation, plus full underlying compare_rasters evidence. ALWAYS use this when the user asks 'should this claim pay out?', 'is the trigger fired?', 'evaluate the insurance', or any parametric-trigger question on drone NDVI data. Source='drone' — for satellite-based triggers use get_insurance_intelligence.

layer_id_before.description: The layer_id of the EARLIER drone NDVI raster ('before' / baseline). For policy onboarding without a prior flight, pass the same id as layer_id_after — degenerate case will be reported.
layer_id_before.title: Layer Id Before

layer_id_after.description: The layer_id of the LATER drone NDVI raster ('after' / current claim flight).
layer_id_after.title: Layer Id After

band.description: Which band contains NDVI (1-indexed). For single-band NDVI use 1; for typical 4-band drone NDVI export use 2.
band.title: Band

crop.description: Crop in the field. Supported: 'maize', 'beans', 'rice', 'sorghum', 'wheat'. Pass 'unknown' if not specified — accuracy will be reduced.
crop.title: Crop

polygon_geojson.description: GeoJSON Polygon string defining the insured field boundary, OR empty string '' to use the whole 'after' raster. Used to compute the current absolute-NDVI signal.
polygon_geojson.title: Polygon Geojson

find_similar_tiles.name: find_similar_tiles
find_similar_tiles.description: Find tiles visually similar to a point in a user's raster, across all of that user's other rasters. Powered by Clay v1.5 visual embeddings in Qdrant. Use this when the user asks 'find other fields that look like this stressed patch', 'have we seen this damage pattern in any other flight?', 'show me similar areas across my orthophotos', or any cross-flight visual similarity question. Returns top-K similar tiles ranked by cosine similarity (1.0 = identical, lower = less similar). For exact-match analysis at a single point use read_pixel_at; for whole-field health verdict use interpret_raster_health. Only works on rgb_visual rasters that have been embedded (orthophotos auto-embed after COG conversion completes).

layer_id.description: The layer_id of the user-uploaded raster the query point lives in.
layer_id.title: Layer Id

longitude.description: Longitude in WGS84 decimal degrees of the query point.
longitude.title: Longitude

latitude.description: Latitude in WGS84 decimal degrees of the query point.
latitude.title: Latitude

top_k.description: How many similar tiles to return. Use 5-10 for a quick scan, up to 50 for a thorough audit. Pass 0 to use the default of 10.
top_k.title: Top K

render_map_snapshot.name: render_map_snapshot
render_map_snapshot.description:
Render a static PNG snapshot of the current map and dispatch it to a delivery channel.

    WHEN TO USE: the user wants a snapshot they can SHARE outside the browser —
    Telegram message, WhatsApp message, email attachment — or wants a permalink
    PNG of the current view. Also use for cron-fired reports to insurance partners.

    WHEN NOT TO USE: the user wants to keep working in the browser (they already
    see the map). Numeric/tabular results — use the specific stats tool. Adding a
    NEW data layer on the map — use display_satellite_layer or compute_spectral_index.

    Delivery model: renders PNG, uploads to MinIO under snapshots/{map_id}/, then
    publishes a payload on the 'mundi:render_snapshot' Redis channel. A separate
    sender process consumes the channel, filters on delivery_channel and partner_id,
    and performs the actual send. Returns once the payload is published; does NOT
    block on the sender. Snapshot URLs are valid for 24 hours.

width.description: Image width in pixels. Use 1024 for chat attachments, 1600 for reports.
width.title: Width

height.description: Image height in pixels. Use 600 for chat, 1000 for reports.
height.title: Height

caption.description: One-line caption shown to the recipient, e.g. 'NDVI Cyampirita 2026-05-12, mean=0.62'.
caption.title: Caption

delivery_channel.description: Where to send the snapshot. 'browser' = the user's current map view. 'telegram'/'whatsapp'/'email' route to an out-of-band sender; recipient must be set for those.
delivery_channel.title: Delivery Channel

recipient.description: Destination for non-browser channels: Telegram chat_id, WhatsApp E.164 number, or email address. Pass an empty string for 'browser'.
recipient.title: Recipient
